ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general instant coffee beverage class; no specific 3-in-1 premix subclass exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers instant coffee powder; the 3-in-1 premix formulation (with creamer and sugar) is not separately coded. Closest parent match documented.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for 3-in-1 instant coffee premix. Left null to preserve ontology honesty.

allergens: may contain milk derivatives (non-dairy creamer may contain casein)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard sachet (approximately 20 g) dissolved in 150–180 ml of hot water constitutes a typical single serving of 3-in-1 flavoured instant coffee.

nutrient_source.title: 3-in-1 Flavoured Coffee — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 3-in-1 flavoured coffee (cr_id D09010008). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 3-in-1 flavoured instant coffee premix in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 53 (low) based on the analogous food 'instant coffee with sugar and non-dairy creamer' from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283), which reports GI ~53 for sweetened instant coffee beverages. Glycemic load per 20 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (53 × 2.05 g) / 100 ≈ 1.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar content (6.36 g/100 g) in a beverage matrix; palm-based non-dairy creamer contributing saturated fat
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid sugar-sweetened coffee premixes; the 6.36 g/100 g sugar load independently elevates cardiovascular risk per AHA 2018 guidelines, and the palm-based creamer adds saturated fat that further burdens vascular health — plain unsweetened coffee is the recommended alternative.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 6.36 g sugar/100 g and low dietary fibre (0.66 g/100 g) causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this product entirely, as the combination of added sugar and negligible fibre in a liquid matrix produces rapid blood glucose elevation; the ADA classifies all sugar-sweetened beverages as unsuitable for T2D management, and unsweetened black coffee or coffee with a non-caloric sweetener should be substituted.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from palm-based non-dairy creamer (~1.9 g/100 g) combined with added sugar (6.36 g/100 g) contributing to atherogenic and inflammatory risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, this ultra-processed premix is inadvisable due to its palm-oil-derived saturated fat content and added sugar, both of which are independently associated with elevated LDL cholesterol and endothelial inflammation; switching to plain unsweetened coffee eliminates both risk factors.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory combination of added sugar and palm-based saturated fat counteracting coffee polyphenol benefit
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: For individuals with rheumatoid arthritis, the added sugar and palm-oil creamer in this premix promote inflammatory cytokine activity and negate the anti-inflammatory polyphenol benefit of coffee; plain unsweetened black coffee is the preferred alternative.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 3-in-1 Flavoured Instant Coffee is an ultra-processed beverage premix widely consumed across Singapore and Southeast Asia. Each sachet contains a pre-blended combination of instant coffee granules, non-dairy creamer (typically palm-oil-based), refined sugar, and additional flavourings. The product is prepared by dissolving one sachet (approximately 20 g) in 150–180 ml of hot water, yielding a sweet, creamy, mildly bitter coffee beverage with a smooth texture. Per 100 g of prepared beverage, it provides 62 kcal, 0.41 g protein, 2.26 g fat (predominantly saturated from palm-based creamer), 10.26 g carbohydrate, 6.36 g sugar, 0.66 g dietary fibre, and 30 mg sodium. Calcium and iron are reported as 0 mg/100 g by HPB Singapore. The product is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) due to its 6.36 g/100 g sugar content, making it clinically unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 53 (low) based on analogous sweetened instant coffee data. It is a staple breakfast and morning beverage in Singaporean hawker culture and is commonly found in convenience stores, vending machines, and office pantries throughout the region. The product is halal-certified in most commercial formulations and is vegetarian but not vegan due to potential casein content in the non-dairy creamer.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a sachet of 3-in-1 coffee?
What is the sugar content of instant coffee premix?
What is the GI of 3-in-1 coffee?
Can I drink flavoured coffee if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09010008) at confidence 0.95. The record is marked as reviewed and canonical. GI is an analogous estimate from an approved peer-reviewed source (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care), which reduces GI confidence to medium but does not affect overall record confidence given the strong primary nutrient source.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this 3-in-1 flavoured coffee premix are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (D09010008) as of 2026-04-16. 2. calcium_mg and iron_mg are reported as 0.0 by HPB Singapore (confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database). 3. saturated_fat_g estimated at 1.9 g/100 g based on the palm-based non-dairy creamer composition typical of this product category, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entry for instant coffee with creamer and sugar (FDC ID 171891); this is an adapted value as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. 4. GI value of 53 is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283) for sweetened instant coffee beverages; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 5. Dietary tags 'low_sodium' and 'low_calorie' from the input are preserved as contextually accurate (30 mg sodium/100 g; 62 kcal/100 g) but are noted as per-100 g values of a prepared beverage, not per-sachet values. 6. The non-dairy creamer in commercial 3-in-1 coffee products commonly contains sodium caseinate (a milk derivative), making the product not fully vegan despite the absence of liquid dairy.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' to align with allowed values.
Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' as GI 53 falls within the 'low' category (GI <= 55) according to University of Sydney GI Database guidelines.
Added 'San He Yi Feng Wei Ka Fei' to the flat 'aliases' array to ensure consistency with 'aliases_by_language.romanized' entry, resolving q8 failure.
